description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The grooves are deep and dark, contrasting with the lighter, marbled stone material visible between them. The block has a solid, robust appearance, with sharp, clean edges and a uniform thickness. The sides of the block are smooth and unadorned, emphasizing the functional, utilitarian design. The coloration is predominantly a mix of dark gray and black, with some lighter, mottled stone textures visible on the grooved surface. This type of block is commonly used as a barrier or obstacle in urban environments, such as on sidewalks, plazas, or as part of a drainage or ventilation system.
description: A rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, featuring a marbled gray and black coloration. The block is solid and robust, with a utilitarian design suited for urban barriers.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block and the parallel grooves running horizontally across the top surface.
description_view_1: Side-angled view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the marbled stone texture more visible between the grooves.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the uniformity of the grooves and the solid, unadorned side surfaces.
description_view_3: Opposite side-angled view, again emphasizing the groove depth and the contrast between the dark grooves and lighter stone.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a drainage or ventilation cover
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with no moss or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: flush with pavement or integrated into a continuous surface
